title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Phoebe, Ross, Joey, and Rachel are there, Joey is demonstrating a card trick.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, pick a card, any card. (Monica picks one) All right, now memorize it. Show to everybody. Got it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um-mm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, give it back to me. (takes the card back, but he looks at the card before he puts it back in the deck, he holds the deck to his forehead, and thinks a little while) 5 of hearts.
Monica is sarcastically amazed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Real magic does exist.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow. Joey, (sarcastically) how do you do it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't tell you that, no.
We hear some knocking coming from the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, somebody's at the door on the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Noo, that's our unbelievably loud upstairs neighbor.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He took up the carpet, and now you can hear everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don't you go up there and ask him to 'step lightly, please?'

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have like five times, but the guy is so charming, that I go up there to yell and then I end up apologizing to him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, that is silly. (gets up) I'll go up there, I'll tell him to keep it down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, be my guest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good luck.
Phoebe exits

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right, all right, all right, all right, you really wanna know how I did it, I'll show ya. When you handed me back the card, what you didn't see was, I looked at it so fast that it was invisible to the naked eye. (picks up a card and quickly looks at it) I just did it. (does it again) I just did it, again. Here, I'll slow it down so that you guys can see it. (looks and the card in slow motion)

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, I got it.
We hear Phoebe knock at the door upstairs, and the guy answer it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (muffled through the floor) Yeah, look I was with my friend downstairs and we hear everything up here that you do, and I am sick and tired... (I tired but the rest is unintelligible).

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (muffled) Whoa, you are very beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (muffled) Oh, thank you.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey, anybody got a length of rope about six feet long with a little nouse at the end?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just saw Janice.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, she was at Rockefeller Center skating with her husband, she looked so happy. I almost feel bad for whipping that kid's pretzel at them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, I remember the first time I saw that girl Katherine, after we broke up. She was just walking with her friend Donna, just laughing and talking. God, it killed me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but you ended up having sex with both of them that afternoon.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry, I just, any excuse to tell that story y'know....

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Chandler, there's a party tomorrow, you'll feel better then.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y'know what, I'm gonna be okay, you don't have to throw a party for me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's Joey's birthday.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well then, if anybody should have a party it should be him.
We hear Phoebe's muffled voice through the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sush!! I cannot believe she is still up there.
We hear the guy telling a joke, and Phoebe laughing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, well he totally screwed up the punch line. Y'know, it's supposed to be arrghh-eh og-errigh.
Fortunata Fashions, Rachel's new job.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: (entering) How's that coffee comin,' dear?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (jumping up from reading her magazine) Yeah, right away Mr. Kaplan.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: I'm not supposed to drink coffee, it makes me gassy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: I'll bet your thinking, 'What's an intelligent girl who wants to be in fashion, doing making coffee?' Eh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: Eh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you got me.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: Well, don't think I haven't noticed your potential. Well, I've got a project for you that's a lot more related to fashion. How does that sound?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that sounds great.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: Come on over here, sweetheart. (they walk over to a storage closet)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh thank you so much Mr. Kaplan, thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Mr. Kaplan
lines.text: (opening the closet door revealing that it's full of tangled up hangers.) I need these hangers separated ASAP. (she is stunned) You're welcome.
The Moondance Diner, Rachel is talking to Monica about her job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God, I hate my job, I hate it, I hate my job, I hate it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know honey, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I wanna quit, but then I think I should stick it out, then I think why would such a person stay in such a demeaning job, just because it's remotely related to the field they're interested in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (gives her a look) Gee, I don't know Rach. Order up!! I got a Yentel soup, a James Beans, and a Howdy hold the Dowdy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh honey, come on, I'm sorry, I didn't.... I don't mind paying my dues, y'know, its just how much am I gonna learn about fashion by walking Mira, the arthritic seamstress, to the bathroom.
A guy at the end of the table starts laughing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to him) Hi! Is my misery amusing to you?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I'm sorry, I wa, I wa, I was just ah.... (starts to laugh again)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not funny, this is actually my job.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Oh believe me, I-I-I've been there. I had to sort mannequin heads at that Mannequins Plus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well then, so I'm just going to go back to talking to my friend here. And you can go back to enjoying your little hamburger.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Ah, just one other thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes?!

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I ah, I work at Bloomingdale's and I might know of a job possibility if your, if your interested?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looks at Monica) (to him) Do you want my pickle?
Chandler and Joey's, it's Joey's party.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (to Monica and Phoebe) Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Gunther. Hi. (to Phoebe) I mean you're going out on a date with the noisy guy upstairs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he's very charming.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, he's too charming, but if you two start going out, then it's just gonna make it so much harder for me to hate him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you're just gonna have to try.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, where are the Jell-o shots?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, Chandler is supposed to be passin' 'em around...
Camera cuts to show Chandler giving a Jello shot to the ceramic dog and holding an empty tray of Jello shots.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello-dillillio!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, somebody's feeling better.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) Stick out your tongue.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) Take off your shirt!
Chandler sticks out is tongue and it's a horrible shade of green.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! How many of these things did you have? These are pure vodka.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, Jell-o just like Mom used to make.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering, to Ross) The most unbelievable thing happened to me today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! So I'm out having lunch at Monica's and this guy starts talking to me, and it turns out he works for a buyer at Bloomingdale's and there happens to be an opening in his department. So I gave him my phone number and he's gonna call me this weekend to see if he can get me an interview!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, so this guy is helping you for no apparent reason?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And he's, he's a total stranger?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! His name is um, Mark something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh. Sounds like Mark Something wants to have some sex.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What!?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'm just saying, I mean why else would he just, y'know, swoop in out of nowhere for no reason.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: To be nice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, Joey. Are men ever nice to strange women for no reason?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, only for sex.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you. (to Rachel) So did you ah, did you tell Mark Something about me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn't have to, because I was wearing my 'I heart Ross' sandwich board and ringing my bell.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sisters
lines.text: (entering) Joey!!! Happy Birthday!! (all 7 of them look almost identical)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!! Hey-hey-hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) Okay, how many of that girl are you seeing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How hammered are you? Huh? These, these are Joey's sisters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi Joey's sisters!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to one of his sisters, Cookie) Hey!

lines.speaker: Cookie
lines.text: Hey. What are we drinkin' over here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I have ah, vodka and cranberry juice.

lines.speaker: Cookie
lines.text: No kiddin,' that's the exact same drink I made myself right after I shot my husband.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow. Okay, I don't know how to talk to you.
Cut to Chandler
Sister 1: (to Chandler) What 'cha doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I'm taking my ex-girlfriend of my speed dialer.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sisters
lines.text: Oh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, no, it's a good thing. Why must we dial so speedily anyway? Why must we rush through life? Why can't we savor the precious moments? (to one of Joey's sisters) Those are some huge breasts you have.
Cut to Ross and Monica

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. So um, I-I heard about this ah, Mark guy that ah, Rachel met today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Isn't that great?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah! So ah, kinda pretty, pretty good. He sounds like a nice, good guy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, he is. And he is so dreamy. I mean, y'know what, when he left I actually used the phrase, 'Hummina-hummina-hummina.' (walks away)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's excellent.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel are eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) So, he's just a nice guy. You really think this Mark doesn't want anything in exchange for helping him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I assume I'll have to take showers with him, but y'know, that's true of any job.
Chandler enters hungover and groaning

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How ya feelin'?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, my apartment isn't there anymore, because I drank it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where'd you get too? We lost you after you opened up all the presents.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I ended up in the storage room, and not alone.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Woooo hoooo!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ow, no 'woo-hooing,' no 'woo-hooing.'

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why, what happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, I fooled around with Joey's sister. (Phoebe gasps) Well, that's not the worst part.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is the worse part?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't remember which sister.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel) You see what men do! Don't tell me men are not nice! (points to Chandler) This is men!!
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you insane? I mean Joey, is going to kill you, he's actually going to kill you dead.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! You don't think I thought of that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How can you not know which one?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean that's unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, was it Gina?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Which one is Gina?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Dark, big hair, with the airplane earrings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, no, no, that's Dina.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Monica) You see you can't tell which one is which either, dwha!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We didn't fool around with any of them. Dwha! Dwha!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Veronica. Look, it's got to be Veronica, the girl in the red skirt. I definitely stuck my tongue down her throat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, when I've been drinking, sometimes I tend to get overly friendly, and I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's all right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (angrily entering, to Chandler) Can I talk to you for a second?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. (they all walk away from Chandler)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on!! (motions for Chandler to come with him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why can't we talk in here? With, with, witnesses.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I just got off the phone with my sister.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, which, which one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know which one was she again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why don't you ask Chandler, 'cause he's the one that fooled around with her. She told me you said you could really fall for her. Now is that true? Or are you just gettin' over Janice by groping my sister.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's gotta be the first one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? That's great! You and my sister, sittin' in a tree.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep, I'm in a tree.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is writing letters as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did he call? Did Mark call?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No honey, I'm sorry, but the weekend's not over yet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. (we hear laughing from the upstairs apartment) Oh my God, is that Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess they're back from their date.
He starts to play music.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Music. Very nice.
We hear them start making out upstairs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, how are you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am good. I finished my book.
Things start to get really hot upstairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, what's it about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't remember. Do you wanna take a walk?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I do. (they both run out)
Chandler and Joey's, Ross is reading a letter that Chandler wrote.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dear Mary-Angela. Hi. How's it going. This is the hardest letter I've ever had to write. (to Chandler) What the hell's a matter with you? How do you think Joey's going to react when he finds out that you blew off his sister with a letter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that's the part where you tell him that I moved to France. When actually I'll be in Cuba.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, look, look, you've got to do this yourself, okay in person. At least you know her name. You just go to the house and you ask for Mary-Angela, okay, when which ever one she is comes to the door, you take her for a walk, you let her down easy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What if Mary-Angela comes to the door and I ask for Mary-Angela?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where in Cuba?
Joey's sister's house, Chandler hits himself on the head three times and knocks on the door three times. Joey answers it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (shocked) Joey, what-wh-wh-wha-wh-wha-wh-wha-wh-wha-what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Waiting for my Grandma to finish my laundry. What about you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm here to see Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You are so the man! (motions him to come in, and he does) Now look, listen, listen, you got to be cool, 'cause my Grandma doesn't know about you two yet, and you do not want to tick her off. She was like the sixth person to spit on Mussolini's hanging body. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Where's Mary-Angela?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She's right in there. (motions to the living room)
Chandler walks into the living room, and sees all of Joey's sisters, all wearing red.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sisters
lines.text: Hey, Chandler!
Central Perk, Monica, Ross, and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe Mark didn't call. It's Sunday night, and he didn't call.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bummer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Yeah, right. Look at you, you're practically giddy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm genuinely sorry the Mark thing didn't work out. Look, Rach, I want only good things for you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, why don't you just call Mark. (they both look up in shock) I mean, who says you have to sit here and wait for him, you've got to make stuff happen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But, you, you don't want to seem too pushy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Ross) Honey, it's not pushy, he gave her his home number.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Rachel, as she gets his number) What, he gave you his home number? As in like, to, to his home?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and you don't mind if I call, because you only want good things for me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right good things, that-that is what I said. (glances at Monica)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Hello, Mark? Hi, it's Rachel Green. (listens) Oh no, don't you apologize. (listens) Yeah, I'll hold. (to Monica and Ross) He left my number at work, but he was helping his niece with her report on the pioneers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That is so made up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, oh my God, tomorrow! That, no, it's perfect. Oh God, thank you soo much. Great! Bye! (hangs up phone) I got the interview!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He even offered to meet me for lunch tomorrow to prep me for it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that is amazing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah well, if I know Mark, and I think I do, I'd expect nothing less.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got to figure out what I'm going to wear.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: High collar and baggy pants say I'm a pro.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Right! Okay, I'll see you guys later. (starts to leave) Woo hoo!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You go get 'em. (to Monica) What did I do to you? Did I hurt you in some way?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Why don't you call him?!' Well, thank you very much! Y'know now he is going to prep her, y'know prep her, as in what you do when you surgically remove the boyfriend!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you crazy?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Am I! Am I! Am I out of my mind! Am I losing my senses!! This dreamy guy is taking my girlfriend out for a meal.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Ross y'know this isn't even about you! I mean this is about Rachel and something wonderful happening for her. I mean you know even if you're right, what if he wants to sleep with her, does it mean he gets too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean don't you trust her?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then get over yourself! Grow up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (shyly) You grow up.
Joey's sister's house, they're finishing up dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This teramisu is, is excellent. Did you make it Mary-Angela?

lines.speaker: Joey's Grandmother
lines.text: No! I did!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's, it's yummy. So Mary-Angela do you like it?

lines.speaker: Joey's Grandmother
lines.text: Of course! It's her favorite.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So um, Mary-Angela, what's your second favorite?

lines.speaker: Joey's Grandmother
lines.text: More of Grandma's teramisu.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Would you just please....give me the receipt 'cause this is great. It's top notch.

lines.speaker: Joey's Grandmother
lines.text: That dies with me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So will I.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: Excuse yourself, and go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no I was just squinting. That doesn't mean anything.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: (whispering in his ear) Just do it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Will you excuse me I have to um..... (walks to the hall)
[Cut to the hall, Joey's sister runs up and grabs Chandler's butt.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (startled) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: Finally, I thought we'd never be alone. Can I just tell you something, I have not stopped thinking about you since the party. (kisses him)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I may have jumped the gun here. (she tries to kiss him, but he ducks it and moves away) Um, I just got out of a relationship and I'm not really in a, in a commitment kind of place.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: So! Me neither! God, Mary-Angela was right you do have the softest lips.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahhhh, you're not Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: No, I'm Mary-Theresa.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is so bad. If-if you're not Mary-Angela, then-then who is?

lines.speaker: Mary-Angela
lines.text: (standing behind Chandler) I am!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, this is soo bad. (doesn't see Mary-Angela)

lines.speaker: Mary-Angela
lines.text: Joey!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No Joey! No Joey! Don't Joey! Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's goin' on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pats him on the shoulder) You're it! Now run and hide!

lines.speaker: Mary-Theresa
lines.text: It's no big deal. Chandler was just kissin' me because he thought I was Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! How could you do that, how could you think she was Mary-Angela?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wasn't sure which one Mary-Angela was. (all of Joey's sisters gasp) Look, I'm sorry okay, I was really drunk, and you all look really similar.

lines.speaker: Joey's Sister
lines.text: I say, punch him Joey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! Punch him!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, we should all calm down because your brother's not going to punch me. (to Joey) Are ya?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that is usually what I would do. But I just never thought you'd be on the receiving end of it. How could you do this?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey if you wanna punch me, go ahead, I deserve it. But I just want you to know that I would never soberly hurt you or your family, you're my best friend. I would never do anything like this ever again.

lines.speaker: Cookie
lines.text: So what. I say, punch him.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! Punch him! Punch him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No! No! No, I'm not going to punch Chandler.

lines.speaker: Cookie
lines.text: I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No you won't. Look he knows he did a terrible thing and I believe him, he's sorry. But, (to Chandler) you've got one more apology to make, all right, you've got to apologize to Mary-Angela.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, absolutely!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You've got it. (he starts to look at his sister's, but he still doesn't know which one is Mary-Angela.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cookie, now you can punch him!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! (Cookie punches him)
A lobby, Ross is waiting for Rachel, after her interview.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (getting of the elevator and noticing Ross) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah y'know, this building is on my paper route so I... (gives her a flower)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. (kisses her)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How'd did it go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well, the woman I interviewed with was pretty tough, but y'know thank God Mark coached me, because once I started talking about the fall line, she got all happy and wouldn't shut up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm so proud of you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, I'm ah, I'm sorry I've been so crazy and jealous and, it's just that I like you a lot, so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (turns around) Hi Mark!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Hi. I just talked to Joanna, and she loves you. You got it, you got the job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I did!

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Oh my God!! (she turns around and hugs Mark, not Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Congratu!! (sees her hug Mark) -lations-lations.
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, The entire gang is there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So ah, Phoebe, how was your date?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh well y'know. (laughs)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I do know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ick, you were eavesdropping.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Eavesdropping. Pheebs, the ceiling tiles were falling down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. But I really like this guy. And I think he really happens to like me.
We hear the sound of a bed creaking through the ceiling, and him moaning.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe he's just jumping on a pogo-stick and really likes it?
We hear a women start moaning.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe the pogo-stick likes it too?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, that's it! He cannot do this to Phoebe. (gets up) This guy is going to get the butt kicking of a lifetime! (stops and turns around and asks Rachel) But, is he a big guy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, we'll all go. (motions to Chandler) Come on. (the guys leave)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks you guys! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't worry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, I hope they kick his ass!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know if it's any consolation, he really did sound like he was having more fun with you. (Rachel nods in agreement)
We the guys knock on the door upstairs, and the guy answering it.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (muffled) May I help you?
We hear the guys start to attack him, but the guy manages to calm them down and gets them to agree to what he did. Monica throws up her hands in disgust.
End
